title: Handshake vs Skylead — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Skylead for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, and multi-sender support side-by-side to decide which platform fits your team.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a LinkedIn automation platform purpose-built for multi-sender rotation. It lets teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard, with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included in every plan. Handshake's pricing is based on the number of sender accounts — not per-seat — so your entire team collaborates without multiplying costs. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale to enterprise volumes.
overview.competitor: Skylead is a cloud-based sales engagement platform that combines LinkedIn automation with email outreach in a single tool. It's known for its 'Smart Sequences' — visual workflows with if/else conditional logic that adapt based on prospect behavior (e.g., send an email if a LinkedIn message goes unanswered). Skylead charges $100/month per seat (one LinkedIn account), with unlimited email accounts included. It also offers image and GIF personalization, a built-in email finder/verifier, and free email warmup. Available since 2019, Skylead targets sales teams, recruiters, and agencies who want multichannel outreach in one platform.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — automatically rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Not available — each seat is a separate LinkedIn account running independent campaigns

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual ramp-up over 3 weeks for new accounts
featureComparison.competitor: No native LinkedIn warmup — offers free email warmup only

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox aggregating conversations from all sender accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Centralized inbox for each seat, but no cross-account unified view

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan — no per-seat cost for team members
featureComparison.competitor: Each team member needs their own $100/mo seat tied to their LinkedIn account

featureComparison.handshake: Standard sequence automation with A/B testing; no conditional branching
featureComparison.competitor: Advanced Smart Sequences with if/else conditions — adapts path based on prospect actions (e.g., accepted connection? send message; no? try email)
Unlimited email accounts, up to 100K emails/month, built-in email finder and verifier

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included, human-like sending patterns, smart rate limiting
featureComparison.competitor: Cloud-based with randomized delays and daily limits; no dedicated residential proxies included
Built-in image and GIF personalization for connection requests and messages

featureComparison.handshake: Advanced analytics with campaign performance, acceptance rates, reply tracking across all senders
featureComparison.competitor: Detailed campaign reports with step-by-step analytics per sequence
Webhooks, Zapier, and open API available

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in A/B testing on all plans

sections.handshakeWins:
Handshake's clear advantage over Skylead is its multi-sender rotation architecture and team-friendly pricing. If you're running outreach across 5 or more LinkedIn accounts, the cost savings are dramatic: $199/mo for 5 senders on Handshake vs. $500/mo on Skylead. At 10 senders, you're saving $700/mo — enough to pay for several additional tools in your stack.

The unified inbox is another major differentiator. On Skylead, each seat operates as its own silo — there's no way to see replies from all LinkedIn accounts in one view. Handshake consolidates every conversation from every sender into a single inbox, which means faster response times and no missed replies.

Handshake also includes premium residential proxies on every plan. Skylead is cloud-based (which is safer than browser extensions), but it doesn't provide dedicated residential proxies — you're relying on Skylead's infrastructure without the same level of IP diversity. For teams managing many accounts, this matters for long-term account safety.

The automated LinkedIn warmup on Handshake is a genuine time-saver. Skylead's warmup feature only covers email accounts, not LinkedIn profiles. When you add a new sender to Handshake, the warmup process is handled automatically over 3 weeks — no manual limit management required.
sections.competitorWins:
Skylead's biggest strength is its multichannel approach. The Smart Sequences with if/else logic are genuinely powerful — you can build workflows where the system automatically switches from LinkedIn to email based on prospect behavior. If someone doesn't accept your connection request within 3 days, Skylead can automatically find their email and follow up there. Handshake doesn't offer this because it's focused purely on LinkedIn.

The built-in email finder and verifier is a real convenience. Skylead can discover and validate business emails directly within your outreach workflow, saving you from needing a separate tool like Hunter or Apollo.

Image and GIF personalization is another area where Skylead stands out. You can send custom images with the prospect's name, company logo, or personalized text embedded — directly from the platform. This kind of visual personalization can boost reply rates by 15-25% in competitive markets.

For solo users who want one tool that handles both LinkedIn and email, Skylead's all-in-one approach at $100/mo is compelling — you get LinkedIn automation, unlimited email, email warmup, and a built-in email finder in a single subscription.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if your outreach is primarily LinkedIn-based and you need to scale across multiple sender accounts. It's ideal for sales teams running multi-sender rotation, agencies managing client campaigns, and any organization where team collaboration (without per-seat pricing) is important. If account safety is a top priority, Handshake's included residential proxies and automated LinkedIn warmup provide a stronger safety layer than Skylead.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Skylead if you need true multichannel outreach combining LinkedIn and email in one workflow. It's a strong choice for solo SDRs or small teams that want if/else conditional sequences, built-in email finding, and image personalization — all in one tool. If you're managing 1-2 LinkedIn accounts and value the email integration, Skylead's $100/mo all-in-one plan offers a lot of functionality.
sections.verdict:
For teams scaling LinkedIn outreach across multiple accounts, Handshake wins on price, safety, and collaboration. At 5 senders, you save $300/mo vs. Skylead — and the gap keeps growing. The unified inbox and multi-sender rotation are features Skylead simply doesn't offer.

Skylead holds its own for users who need multichannel LinkedIn + email automation in a single platform, especially if Smart Sequences and visual personalization are priorities. But if LinkedIn is your primary channel and you need to manage multiple accounts efficiently, Handshake delivers significantly more value.

faqs.question: Is Handshake cheaper than Skylead?
faqs.answer: Yes — particularly for teams. Handshake's Growth plan offers 5 senders for $199/mo vs. $500/mo on Skylead (5 × $100). Handshake also includes unlimited team seats at no extra cost, while Skylead charges per seat.

faqs.question: Does Skylead support multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: No. Each Skylead seat operates independently with its own LinkedIn account. There's no built-in feature to distribute leads across multiple senders or rotate outreach automatically.

faqs.question: Does Skylead offer LinkedIn warmup?
faqs.answer: Skylead offers free email warmup for cold email accounts, but it does not have a dedicated LinkedIn account warmup feature. You'd need to manually manage LinkedIn activity ramp-up. Handshake automates LinkedIn warmup over 3 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I switch from Skylead to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes. Connect your LinkedIn accounts to Handshake and set up campaigns. No data migration is needed since LinkedIn data stays on the platform. Most teams get running within a day.

faqs.question: Which tool is safer for my LinkedIn account?
faqs.answer: Both are cloud-based, which is safer than browser extensions. However, Handshake includes premium residential proxies matched to each sender's geographic location and automated warmup scheduling — both of which provide an extra layer of safety that Skylead doesn't include by default.
